name: TransformCoord
summary: This class offers functions to transform a world point into the local coordinate system (PointLocal) defined by a geometry object or a vector of objects. Functions named PointGlobal will take a local point and project its coordinates into world coordinates.

methods.name: PointGlobal(path, localPoint, eps)
methods.summary: Transform the local coordinates to world coordinates.
methods.remarks: Transform the local coordinates to world coordinates.

params.name: localPoint
params.doc: Local point with offset as x and distance as y

methods.summary: Transform the local offset to world coordinates.

methods.name: PointGlobal(line, offset)
methods.summary: Transform the local coordinates to world coordinates. emarks If the object is a 3D line, the correct result would be a 3D circle around the line. In this case this function returns the intersection point between the circle and a plane. Where the plane is defined by the xy axis and the same z value as the circles center point.
methods.remarks: Transform the local coordinates to world coordinates. emarks If the object is a 3D line, the correct result would be a 3D circle around the line. In this case this function returns the intersection point between the circle and a plane. Where the plane is defined by the xy axis and the same z value as the circles center point.

methods.name: PointGlobal(spline, offset, eps)
methods.summary: Transform the local coordinates to world coordinates. emarks Due to restrictions in the old functions (splin7, splin2) local points with a x value smaller than 0 are treated as x=0. If the x value of the local point is bigger than the splines length, the length of the spline is uses as x value.
methods.remarks: Transform the local coordinates to world coordinates. emarks Due to restrictions in the old functions (splin7, splin2) local points with a x value smaller than 0 are treated as x=0. methods.name: PointGlobalEx
methods.summary: Transform the local coordinates to world coordinates. This method compute global point by offset related to axis of clothoid (clothoid with parallel=0). This method is faster and computing global point with higher precision. Use PointGlobalEx with PointLocalEx cooperation only. sa PointLocalEx, TransformClothoidLocalOffset
methods.remarks: Transform the local coordinates to world coordinates. This method compute global point by offset related to axis of clothoid (clothoid with parallel=0). This method is faster and computing global point with higher precision. Use PointGlobalEx with PointLocalEx cooperation only. sa PointLocalEx, TransformClothoidLocalOffset

params.name: offset
params.doc: Distance from start point on axis curve

returns.doc: Global point on the geometry by offset on axis curve,

methods.name: PointLocalEx
methods.summary: Calculate the local coordinates on a 2D clothoid PointLocalEx compute local point related to axis of clothoid (clothoid with parallel=0). This method is faster and computing X coordinate with higher precision. Use PointGlobalEx with PointLocalEx cooperation only. PointGlobalEx, TransformClothoidLocalOffset
methods.remarks: Calculate the local coordinates on a 2D clothoid PointLocalEx compute local point related to axis of clothoid (clothoid with parallel=0). This method is faster and computing X coordinate with higher precision. Use PointGlobalEx with PointLocalEx cooperation only. PointGlobalEx, TransformClothoidLocalOffset

returns.doc: Axis local point on the 2D clothoid,

methods.name: TransformClothoidLocalOffset
methods.summary: Transform local offset between clothoid axis curve and parallel curve This function provides transformation between offset related to axis or parallel(real) curve. This function is useful when you have offset on parallel curve and want to get length of axis curve. If clothoid parameter Parallel is zero, then axis and parallel offsets are equal. Sample: calc real length of clothoid ndouble real_length = Service::Length(clot); double curve_length = TransformClothoidLocalOffset(clot, clot.Getlength(), false); real_length and curve_length are equal.
methods.remarks: Transform local offset between clothoid axis curve and parallel curve This function provides transformation between offset related to axis or parallel(real) curve. This function is useful when you have offset on parallel curve and want to get length of axis curve. If clothoid parameter Parallel is zero, then axis and parallel offsets are equal. Sample: calc real length of clothoid ndouble real_length = Service::Length(clot); double curve_length = TransformClothoidLocalOffset(clot, clot.Getlength(), false); real_length and curve_length are equal.

params.name: toAxis
params.doc: If true, then offset is offset on parallel curve and result will be offset on axis curve.

returns.doc: Offset on axis or parallel curve, dependent on toAxis.
